category_title: Young child with severe autism exhibiting physical aggression to escape demands or gain attention
minimal_context: Lauren is sitting alongside her classmates in her special education classroom, tasked with stacking rings independently while the teacher sits next to her.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Here are the rings to stack. I need to complete some work. (Turns body away from the student)

STUDENT: Ring.

TUTOR: Keep stacking.

STUDENT: Look.

TUTOR: I am working right now.

STUDENT: (Screams, grabs the teacher, and begins pushing and kicking)

options.A: Turn toward the student and issue a statement of concern, such as 'What do you need?' along with brief physical contact, like a pat on the back.
options.B: Implement tick charts, token systems, and 'first-then' strategies where the teacher monitors rather than directs the student during the activity.
options.C: Instruct the students to play with anything, using any material available in the classroom, such as picture cards or rings.
options.D: Ask the student to select which rings she wants to stack first or which picture cards she would like to sort during the activity.
